verifiedBehavior.fact: Folders have dedicated TileFolderViewModel and TileFolderRegionViewModel types; they are not ordinary adjacent tiles.

Creating a folder is a group-level rearrange branch gated by ITileViewModel::IsHostableByFolder. It creates a dedicated collection, moves the target and dragged tile models into it, and constructs a TileFolderViewModel at the resolved bounds.
Dragging onto an existing folder uses a separate add-to-folder path and records TileAddedToFolder activity.
Folder children are hosted by a dedicated folder region. Insert and remove operations change hosted state and emit element-change notifications.
An empty folder region is deleted. The inspected branch checks for zero children; no binary evidence was found for automatically dissolving a one-child folder.
The expanded folder is an inline folder region with its own nested tile panel, separators, and editable folder name.

unresolved: The exact native center-target tolerance beyond the verified bounds hit test.
The maximum folder child count, if any.

Inline expansion geometry and child drag-out are deferred until a runtime sample is captured; the first implementation batch covers creation, add-to-folder, persistence, rollback, and folder preview rendering.
